channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Trump's NIH director isn't the only official wearing multiple hats during the president's second term
item.description: President Donald Trump has tasked several Cabinet members and other officials with multiple roles in his second administration, most notably Secretary...

item.title: Troubled Nvidia ally Supermicro pledged to hire a new CFO ‘immediately.’ That was 14 months ago.
item.description: “No one wants this job,” Cowen Partners search firm president Shawn Cole said.

item.title: From slave roots to Senate race: Allen West bets on Wesley Hunt's Cinderella story
item.description: If you're looking for a Cinderella story in the Texas GOP Senate race, retired Lt. Col. Allen West thinks former Apache helicopter pilot Wesley Hunt m...

item.title: Cuba is quickly nearing a point of no return as the U.S. weaponizes its Venezuelan oil supplies
item.description: A U.S. resolution in Cuba may not require full regime change, as the Cuban leadership rapidly runs out of options.

item.title: North Korea opens major party congress with Kim emphasizing economy
item.description: North Korea has opened its most important political event in which leader Kim Jong Un is expected to map out his domestic and foreign policy agenda fo...

item.title: Buttigieg, Newsom, AOC top three in new 2028 poll in key presidential primary state
item.description: Pete Buttigieg leads early 2028 presidential polling in New Hampshire at 20%, ahead of Gavin Newsom and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 15% each.

item.title: Ex-Victoria’s Secret CEO’s lawyer caught on hot mic jokingly threatening to ‘kill’ him at Epstein deposition
item.description: Ex-Victoria’s Secret CEO Les Wexner’s lawyer was caught on a hot mic jokingly threatening to “kill" him if he continued giving long answers during his...

item.title: When Bitcoin prices turned against Michael Saylor, he quietly pivoted to risky financial gambit at Strategy
item.description: Saylor has been offseting the Bitcoin drag by reverting to different and dangerous scheme: Issuing tons of preferred stock.

item.title: Red state auditor's report flags Democratic governor's 'concerning' spending on 'luxury' expenditures
item.description: Kentucky Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear is facing heat from the state's Republican auditor, who released a report outlining questionable spending from t...

item.title: As Gates’ Epstein connection unnerves staff, his foundation looks for a way forward
item.description: The billionaire’s abrupt withdrawal from India’s AI Summit follows renewed scrutiny over his relations with Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Cambodian PM says Thai forces occupying disputed land despite Trump-brokered ceasefire
item.description: Trump's Cambodia-Thailand ceasefire is under strain with Thai forces allegedly occupy Cambodian territory, displacing 80,000 locals, according to offi...

item.title: Mamdani’s Utopian Vision Faces Reality
item.description: The socialist superstar is already threatening to hike the property taxes of middle-class New Yorkers.

item.title: Judge Blocks Virginia Dems’ ‘Unconstitutional’ Power Grab — For Now
item.description: The ruling temporarily halts what RNC Chair Joe Gruters called 'an illegal redistricting scheme that a court has already called a blatant abuse of pow...

item.title: America’s Earliest Outdoor Photographs Shown at Hartford’s Wadsworth Atheneum
item.description: Your critic wanted to see this lovely show so much that he put his contempt for the museum’s trustees in abeyance for a day.

item.title: There Is No ‘Moderate’ Solution To The Extreme Immigration Problem Democrats Created
item.description: The New York Times’ The Daily podcast mistakenly interviewed a reasonable voter on immigration last week, so it looks like they’ve swung hard in the o...

item.title: ‘Greatest Country In The World’: USA Hockey Player Promotes Patriotism After OT Win
item.description: Some American athletes may be predisposed to trash their country on the world stage while competing in the 2026 Winter Olympics. But the same can’t be...

item.title: Ed O’Keefe’s Trump Derangement Is So Bad, Even The White House Press Corps Is Laughing At Him
item.description: CBS News’ Ed O’Keefe learned a new concept Wednesday: facts. During Wednesday’s White House press briefing, O’Keefe asked White House Press Secretary ...

item.title: Pharma Ad Scrutiny a Test for Free Speech
item.description: Don’t let Washington decide what Americans are allowed to hear.

item.title: New Trump administration order could lead to the detention of thousands of legal refugees
item.description: The Trump administration has issued a sweeping new order that could lead to the arrest of tens of thousands of refugees who are lawfully in the United...

item.title: Bringing Back The Talking Filibuster Could Do More To Save America Than Just Passing The SAVE Act
item.description: The talking filibuster is worth trying. It is worth a public accounting of whether this Senate, in this moment, for this American people, can deliver ...

item.title: Sue-Pervised Retirement
item.description: Rampant litigation against 401(k) sponsors is narrowing employees’ investment choices and eroding their retirement wealth.

item.title: Virginia judge blocks referendum vote on Democrats' redistricting plan
item.description: A circuit court judge in Virginia issued an emergency temporary injunction Thursday to stop early voting on the Democrats' redistricting ballot refere...

item.title: In Politics, Social Media Has Supplanted Experience
item.description: The clip is the product. Governance is incidental.

item.title: Former Prince Andrew was arrested. Bill Gates backed away from a speech. For these power players, the Epstein walls are closing in
item.description: Many cite the need to avoid further “distraction” as they step down.

item.title: Large banner featuring Trump's face is displayed on Justice Department headquarters
item.description: A large banner featuring Donald Trump's face was hung on the exterior of Justice Department headquarters on Thursday in a physical display of the Repu...

item.title: What the Market Knows That WACC Doesn’t
item.description: Market-implied discount rates reveal how investors price risk, often diverging from WACC and reshaping capital decisions. The post What the Market Kno...

item.title: Attention Bias in AI-Driven Investing
item.description: AI tools may favor popular stocks over overlooked ones, embedding attention bias into investment decisions. The post Attention Bias in AI-Driven Inves...

item.title: Social Security Claiming Strategies for High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Social Security claiming is a capital-allocation decision for affluent clients. This analysis weighs taxes, longevity risk, and liquidity trade-offs. ...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...
